data.description:
## Why

Both link bugs were invisible in telemetry: the batch outcome reports mutation-level `error=None` and "applied N of N" but never the per-sub-op counts, so a link/comment/label that silently no-ops (or fails non-fatally, logged only to stderr) leaves the pass green. The inbound applier already returns `links_applied`; the outbound batch path does not. Make a silent no-op fail loudly.

## What

- Surface `links_applied`, `comments_applied`, `labels_applied` in the outbound batch outcome and the `RECON:` line (parity with the inbound applier's existing `links_applied`).
- Add a **silent-no-op canary**: when a mutation carries N sub-ops of a kind but `applied == 0`, that is a failure condition (`computed > 0 && applied == 0`), surfaced in the outcome/telemetry and asserted by a test that would have caught the link drop.
- No change to sync semantics — telemetry and one invariant only.

## Scope

- In: outcome/telemetry plumbing in `batch_dispatch.py` / `applier.py` / `reconcile.py`; the canary test.
- Out: the probe wiring (story F); applier consolidation (story D).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The outbound batch outcome and `RECON:` line surface `links_applied`, `comments_applied`, `labels_applied`.
- [ ] A test asserts that a mutation with computed sub-ops > 0 but applied == 0 fails (reproduces what the link drop would have triggered).
- [ ] Sync semantics are unchanged (telemetry-only); full reconciler suite green.
- [ ] The canary asserts the count invariant, not specific log strings (anti-change-detector).
data.title: Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary
